title: malva alcea subsp. bismalva
intro: Malva alcea subsp. bismalva, also known as Great Mallow, is a plant species belonging to the Malvaceae family, characterized by its large, velvety leaves and fragrant flowers. Traditionally, this plant has been utilized for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and demulcent effects, which can help soothe digestive issues and skin irritations. The health benefits of Malva alcea subsp. bismalva include alleviating symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, reducing inflammation in the body, and promoting wound healing. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, allowing for flexible application in traditional medicine practices.
